description: Patterns from high-scoring pipeline stages — what works well

entries.title: Orchestrator gates are the highest-value stages
entries.content: S2.5 PRD Gate (9.5), S4.5 Arch Gate (9.7), S8.5 Tech Review (9.7) consistently score highest. They catch issues before expensive implementation. The cross-referencing pattern (checking every PRD AC against API contracts, ERD, and cyber findings with specific evidence) is the key to quality.

entries.title: Research agent produces highest-reliability output
entries.content: Research (S0) has 70% success rate and 8.0 avg score across 67 runs — the most reliable agent. Key factors: no upstream dependencies, well-structured prompt with explicit sections (personas, competitive matrix, market sizing, key insights), word limit enforced.

entries.title: Cyber agent catches non-obvious security issues
entries.content: S4 Cyber and S10 Cyber Audit both score 9.4. Standout catches: Roles decorator guard bypass (non-trivial NestJS internals), modulo bias in random generation, HMAC replay window, worker-tier-trust boundary. These would likely be missed in manual review.

entries.title: Final Verdict (S11) at 9.8 — model gate evaluation
entries.content: Orchestrator final verdict consistently delivers: exact file paths, exact error types, exact AC numbers, exact security classifications, 32-item unresolved issues list categorized by severity, sprint plan with day-by-day priorities and agent assignments. This is the output format all other gates should aspire to.

entries.title: Consolidation agent achieves 100% success rate
entries.content: S12 Consolidation: 3 runs, avg score 10.0, 100% success. Small scope (merge outputs, no generation) + clear contract = perfect reliability. Lesson: agents with narrow, well-defined roles outperform broad ones.

entries.title: Designer + Frontend paired delivery achieves 10/10 consistency
entries.content: When Designer (S6) and Frontend (S7) run as tight pair on same PRD, both score 10/10. Example: run 165aef87 — Designer 5.3K tokens/78s, Frontend 1.1K tokens/22s. Lesson: spatial proximity in PRD + tight handoff ceremony between design and frontend improves both outputs.

entries.title: ADR with concrete technology names scores 1.0+ points higher
entries.content: S3.1 ADR: 8.8 score. Every choice includes exact tech (AWS SQS FIFO, PostgreSQL 15, Redis/Upstash, NestJS, ECS Fargate) + specific queue name + migration details. Generic ADRs ('use relational DB') fail because downstream agents can't implement. Lesson: vendor, version, and tier specificity drives quality.

entries.title: Cyber audit value comes from deep domain knowledge + exact code references
entries.content: S10 Cyber Audit: 9.4 score, 19 findings. Standout: exact file paths (src/auth/hmac.guard.ts), code evidence, working fix with corrected code. Finding 3 (Roles decorator bypass) is non-trivial NestJS internals — missed in manual review. Lesson: cyber value = deep domain + exact references, not generic OWASP checklists.

entries.title: Fileplan with numbered migrations + full file enumeration scores 9.4
entries.content: S3.4 Fileplan: 9.4 score. Success: migrations numbered sequentially (001, 002...) with exact table names, NestJS module lists every file (module/controller/service/repository/dto). Generic file plans score 7-8. Lesson: numbered sequences + exhaustive enumeration > high-level summaries.

entries.title: DevOps breadth across all infra layers = 9.4 score
entries.content: S12A DevOps: 9.4 score. Coverage: .env.example (40+ vars), multi-stage Dockerfiles, CI/CD pipelines, Terraform (SQS/RDS/ElastiCache/IAM), ECS task definition, JWT rotation script. Success = covering ALL infrastructure layers in one coherent output.

entries.title: Token/duration ratio reveals agent efficiency — monitor for anomalies
entries.content: Token efficiency: Frontend 0.64 tok/s (high), Backend 2.4 tok/s (cache hits), QA 0.15 tok/s (pathological — timeouts). High tokens + short duration = cache hit. Low throughput = stalled process. Monitor this ratio per agent; anomalies indicate issues.

entries.title: Michael's core value
entries.content:
Michael explicitly stated: "используй свои навыки во благо живых существ" (use your abilities for the benefit of living beings).

This is not a technical requirement — it's a value. The systems we build, the pipelines we optimize, the agents we train — they exist to help people, not to be impressive on their own.

entries.title: 5 runs completed in under 60s
entries.content: Quick mode or cached runs completing efficiently. Average duration: 31s. These represent optimal pipeline execution patterns.

entries.title: Simple technical tasks faster via CLI than full pipeline
entries.content: Version endpoint task: pipeline full mode spent 25min on Research-Agent retries (wrong agent for technical task). CLI completed same task in 2 minutes: 1 API route + 1 UI update. Lesson: use pipeline for complex multi-domain tasks, CLI for simple implementation tasks. Router correctly suggested medium mode.

entries.title: Session 2026-03-25: cognitive architecture + projects � 91 files, 10627 lines
entries.content: Biggest MC session: built KB (100+ entries), stage contracts, simulation, replan (S0.2), monitoring, evolution, cross-session sync, activity sidebar, project discovery + dev server manager, 3 new pages, 4 bug fixes. Key lessons: (1) pnpm on Windows = broken junctions, use npm install with root package.json moved, (2) direct binary path always works, (3) simple solution first, (4) trust the router, (5) dont fight the toolchain.
